commit.message:
Tilt-based gouge asymmetry for V-gouge and U-gouge

Pen tilt now creates asymmetric cuts: tilting the pen sideways
relative to the stroke direction makes one side of the gouge wider
and the other narrower, like a real chisel held at an angle.

- Store tiltX/tiltY separately on each stroke point (backward
  compatible  old strokes without tiltX render symmetrically)
- computeRibbonD computes cross-tilt by projecting pen tilt vector
  onto the stroke normal at each point
- V-gouge: tiltStrength 0.5 (more angular, dramatic asymmetry)
- U-gouge: tiltStrength 0.35 (rounder, subtler asymmetry)
- Fine tool: tiltStrength 0 (always symmetric)
- Tilt smoothed with alpha 0.4 to avoid jumpiness

commit.message:
Fix critical PerfView collector reliability issues

Three root causes for PerfView collection failures:

1. Stale ETW sessions after force-kill: When PerfView was killed by
   StopTimeoutSec, orphaned 'NT Kernel Logger' and 'PerfViewSession'
   ETW sessions blocked subsequent runs. Added logman cleanup in both
   Start-Collector (pre-flight) and Stop-Collector (post force-kill).

2. MaxCollectSec (90s) < k6 duration (120s): PerfView auto-stopped
   30s before the load test ended, missing the stress-load phase.
   Increased to 150s (120s k6 + 30s margin).

3. Dead alloc-types code in CPU export: Ran PerfView GCStats (up to
   300s timeout) to produce alloc-types.json that was always empty
   the code's own comment acknowledged 'GCStats does not export it'.
   Removed ~120 lines of dead code.

Also added stale intermediate file cleanup (.etl, .kernel.etl, etc.)
before starting collection to prevent conflicts from interrupted runs.

commit.message:
fix: Auto-reinitialize client after failed reconnect + observe fire-and-forget DB tasks (#333)

## Problem

When a session's connection to the copilot CLI is lost and the reconnect
path in `SendPromptAsync` also fails, `_client` is set to `null` and
`IsInitialized` to `false`. Subsequent prompt attempts hit
`GetClientForGroup` which immediately throws **"Service not
initialized"** — before the client-recreation logic can run. This leaves
**ALL sessions permanently dead** until the user manually reconnects via
Settings.

**Crash log evidence:**
```
[ERROR] 'MEAI Hack' reconnect+retry failed, clearing IsProcessing
```
```
[TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException]
StreamJsonRpc.ConnectionLostException: The JSON-RPC connection with the remote party was lost
```

## Root Cause

In the reconnect path of `SendPromptAsync`,
`GetClientForGroup(sessionGroupId)` is called before the client
re-creation logic. When `_client == null` (from a previous failure), it
throws immediately, skipping the reconnection code entirely.

## Fix

### 1. Lazy client re-initialization (CopilotService.cs)
- Restart the persistent server if needed
- Create and start a new client
- Restore `IsInitialized = true`

This lets sessions self-heal on the next prompt attempt instead of being
permanently dead.

### 2. SafeFireAndForget for DB tasks (CopilotService.Utilities.cs + 16
call sites)
Replaced all `_ = _chatDb.AddMessageAsync(...)` and `_ =
_chatDb.UpdateReasoningContentAsync(...)` fire-and-forget calls with
`SafeFireAndForget(...)` — a static helper that observes the task via
`ContinueWith(OnlyOnFaulted)`. While `AddMessageAsync` has internal
`catch(Exception)`, this prevents `UnobservedTaskException` if the catch
is ever narrowed (historical regression pattern).

## Tests Added

- **SafeFireAndForget behavior** — completed, faulted, async-faulted,
and canceled tasks
- **Lazy re-init structural guards** — verify the guard exists before
`GetClientForGroup`, attempts server restart, and skips codespace
sessions
- **ChatDatabase broad-catch guard** — verify `AddMessageAsync` uses
`catch (Exception`
- **All ChatDb calls use SafeFireAndForget** — no bare `_ = _chatDb.`
patterns remain
- **Recovery scenario** — persistent failure → demo recovery → sessions
work

All 2305 tests pass. Mac Catalyst build succeeds.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Serialize gate step builds through XcodeBuildQueue

Gate steps (Build Check in Full Implementation template) were executing
xcodebuild directly via runShellCommand() without going through the
global XcodeBuildQueue actor. When multiple parallel worktrees hit their
Build Check gate simultaneously, concurrent xcodebuild processes would
collide and crash the app.

Now gate/deterministic shell steps detect build commands using
XcodeBuildQueue.isBuildCommand() and route them through
XcodeBuildQueue.shared.withSlot, matching the existing serialization
in TerminalToolsHandler for terminal.run builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix screenshot scripts failing to connect due to missing auth setup (#2285)

`screenshot-all.mjs`, `screenshot-add-data.mjs`, and
`screenshot-section.mjs` fill the Elasticsearch URL then immediately
click **Connect**, but the connection dialog defaults to API Key auth—so
the button stays disabled without credentials, timing out every run.

- Add `await page.getByRole("tab", { name: "No Auth" }).click()` before
clicking Connect in all three scripts, matching the existing pattern in
`screenshot-feature.mjs`
- `screenshot-section.mjs` previously only selected "No Auth" in
`--live` mode; now selects it unconditionally since the form validation
requires it regardless

```js
await page.getByRole("textbox", { name: "Elasticsearch URL" }).fill(esUrl);
await page.getByRole("tab", { name: "No Auth" }).click();        // was missing
await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Connect", exact: true }).click();
```

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2279

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

1. blobSas.ts: expiresOn for the SAS token was computed before fetching the
   (potentially cached) delegation key. Azure Storage requires the SAS expiry
   to be <= the delegation key's signedExpiresOn. With a 7-day cached key, any
   SAS generated after the initial cache prime would have an expiry slightly
   beyond the key's validity, causing Azure to return 403 on every image load.
   Fix: fetch the delegation key first, then cap expiresOn at
   min(now + TTL, delegationKey.signedExpiresOn).

2. importHandler.ts (force-location path): imageUrl was set directly from
   message.attachments.first()?.url — a Discord CDN URL that expires in ~24h.
   uploadImageToBlob was never called. Fix: call uploadImageToBlob the same way
   pinProcessor does, falling back to existingPin.imageUrl if no attachment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove invalid `forwardRef` from preview directory table row (#3018) Wave was emitting React’s `forwardRef render functions accept exactly two parameters` warning on startup with no useful stack trace. The warning came from the preview directory row component being wrapped in `React.forwardRef` even though it neither accepted nor used a forwarded ref. - **Root cause** - `frontend/app/view/preview/preview-directory.tsx` defined `TableRow` with `React.forwardRef(...)`, but the render function was effectively a plain props-only component. - **Change** - Removed the unnecessary `forwardRef` wrapper from `TableRow`. - Kept the component behavior unchanged; it still uses its internal drag ref wiring for DnD. - **Impact** - Eliminates the startup warning. - Aligns the component definition with its actual usage: callers render `TableRow` as a normal component and do not pass refs. ```tsx // before const TableRow = React.forwardRef(function ({ row, idx, ...props }: TableRowProps) { return
...
; }); // after function TableRow({ row, idx, ...props }: TableRowProps) { return
...
; } ``` --- ✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for you](https://github.com/wavetermdev/waveterm/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot) — coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for your repo. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: sawka <2722291+sawka@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Precompute unassigned primary count and index set in ShardSummary (#2287)

`shards.unassigned.primary.present` rebuilds `new
Set(unassignedPrimaries.map(s => s.index).filter(Boolean))` on every
evaluation, even though the summary is already computed in a single
pass. This replaces the intermediate `CatShardRecord[]` with precomputed
aggregates.

- **`ShardSummary` interface**: `unassignedPrimaries: CatShardRecord[]`
→ `unassignedPrimaryCount: number` + `unassignedPrimaryIndices:
Set(string)`
- **`getShardSummary` loop**: increments count and adds to Set inline
instead of collecting records
- **`evaluate`**: consumes precomputed values directly, no per-call
allocation

```diff
-  unassignedPrimaries: CatShardRecord[];
+  unassignedPrimaryCount: number;
+  unassignedPrimaryIndices: Set(string);
```

Shard check median drops from 0.40ms → 0.03ms on the 50k-shard
benchmark. All 109 existing health check tests pass unchanged.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2280

repository.name: openmrs-module-legacyui
repository.description: The legacy user interface for OpenMRS Platform 2.x is chiefly comprised of administrative functions and the patient dashboard. Apparently, a new and more contemporary UI has been introduced via a UI framework and the legacy UI is kept around for administrative functions that are not yet implemented in the new UI. To retire the Legacy UI as planned, it is required to move the implementations and modules that still rely on it in order to maintain backwards compatibility. The main idea behind this project is to move legacy UI functions into an OpenMRS module that these implementations can install until they are able to migrate away from it, since most of the implementations of OpenMRS around the world are running OpenMRS 1.9.

commit.message:
feat: add iNav flight controller support

- Accept iNav (b'INAV') alongside Betaflight (b'BTFL') in FC detection
- Variant-aware DATAFLASH_READ parsing: Betaflight uses 7-byte header
  (addr+len+compression+data), iNav uses simple format (addr+data)
- Suppress Huffman compression for non-Betaflight FCs
- Skip deprecated MSP_BLACKBOX_CONFIG for iNav (returns zeros)
- Dynamic directory naming: fc_{variant}_uid-* instead of fc_BTFL_uid-*
- FCNotBetaflight renamed to FCNotSupported (alias kept for compat)
- 7 new tests (166 total): iNav detection, parsing, sync flow
- Full compatibility analysis at docs/FC_COMPATIBILITY_ANALYSIS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 22 organic and 19 unknown composition materials

Add 22 new organic chemistry materials (IDs 1077-1098) including
simple compounds (Formaldehyde, FormicAcid, Urea, etc.) and
polymers (Polyurethane, Polycarbonate, Nylon, ABS, Kevlar).

Add 19 new unknown composition materials (IDs 1656-1674) including
geological materials (Chalk, Pumice, Slate, Limestone, etc.),
organic unknowns (Peat, Bitumen, Latex, Rosin, etc.), and
a gemstone (Amber).

Add corresponding declarations in Materials.java.
